ontology.foodex2_description: Chapati / Roti - unleavened flat bread made from whole wheat flour

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard phulka (chapati) weighs approximately 35 g and is typically served as part of a meal alongside dal, curry, or vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Chapati, without fat — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017 revised edition)
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Phulka is the puffed, fat-free variant of chapati cooked on a tawa and finished over a flame. ICMR-NIN reports chapati without fat as the closest exact match. Values are per 100 g edible portion.

gi_source.title: Chapati, whole wheat — Glycemic Index Database, University of Sydney
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 52 (low) reported for whole wheat chapati in the University of Sydney GI Database. GL per serving calculated as: (52 × 61.0 × 35 g / 100) / 100 = 11.1 g. Phulka is a fat-free puffed chapati; GI is consistent with whole wheat unleavened flatbread.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~5 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Chapati (phulka) is very low in sodium at approximately 5 mg per 100 g, making it a suitable staple for individuals managing hypertension, provided it is not served with high-sodium accompaniments such as pickles or heavily salted curries.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI 52) and moderate dietary fibre (3.9 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 52 and meaningful dietary fibre from whole wheat atta, chapati (phulka) produces a moderate postprandial glucose response; portion control to 1–2 phulkas per meal is recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes to manage glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and whole grain fibre
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Chapati (phulka) contains minimal saturated fat (~0.4 g per 100 g), zero cholesterol, and whole wheat fibre that supports LDL reduction, making it a heart-friendly staple when consumed without added ghee or butter.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chapati (also spelled chapathi) is a traditional South Asian unleavened flatbread made from whole wheat flour (atta), water, and a pinch of salt. Phulka is the lightest, fat-free variant: the dough is rolled into thin discs and cooked on a dry tawa (cast-iron griddle) until partially cooked, then held directly over an open flame or placed on a hot surface to puff up into a balloon shape, giving it a soft, slightly smoky interior and lightly charred exterior. Each phulka weighs approximately 30–40 g. Nutritionally, chapati is a whole grain staple providing approximately 297 kcal, 9 g protein, 61 g carbohydrate, and 3.9 g dietary fibre per 100 g. Its glycemic index of 52 (low) makes it preferable to refined white bread or plain rice for blood glucose management. The high fibre content supports digestive health and satiety. Chapati is naturally vegan, dairy-free, and halal, and is a cornerstone of everyday Indian, Pakistani, and Singaporean Indian cuisine. It is commonly served alongside dal (lentil soup), sabzi (vegetable curry), paneer dishes, or meat curries. In Singapore, chapati is widely available at Indian restaurants and hawker stalls, often paired with fish curry or chicken curry.
common_user_queries: How many calories in one chapati?
Is phulka the same as chapati?
What is the GI of chapati?
Is chapati good for weight loss?
What flour is used in chapati?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017), which is the primary approved source for Indian dishes. GI of 52 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for whole wheat chapati. Phulka is the fat-free puffed variant of chapati; the ICMR-NIN chapati-without-fat entry is an exact match.
notes: Phulka is the fat-free puffed variant of chapati; no oil or ghee is added during cooking. ICMR-NIN chapati without fat is the exact nutritional match. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN source for this fat-free preparation. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-based whole wheat product with no animal-derived ingredients. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; whole wheat flour contains negligible vitamin C. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a vegan plant food with no animal-derived ingredients. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this entry. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this entry. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this entry. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this entry. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this entry. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this entry. GI category is low (GI 52 ≤ 55 per Veritas threshold rules). GL per serving = (52 × 61.0 × 35) / 10000 = 11.1 g. Serving size of 35 g corresponds to one standard phulka. Source from field survey data (Zaffron Tandoori - Boat Quay) preserved as provenance context only; nutrient values are not derived from the delivery platform.
